title: dactylorhiza × nummiana
intro: Dactylorhiza × nummiana, also known as the nummular orchid or nummular-spotted marsh orchid, is a hybrid orchid species derived from the cross between the Dactylorhiza maculata and Dactylorhiza sambucina. Traditionally, Dactylorhiza × nummiana has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Dactylorhiza × nummiana is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use is not widely documented in modern medicine.
